channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: With plans in the works and troops on the way, military assumes a mostly familiar role on the border
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Dozens of Marines unfurled coils of concertina wire — one on the ground and one slightly above — making it more difficult to climb a border wall separating Tijuana from San Diego.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Breakout Sundance films and key takeaways from this year's festival
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — Film wasn’t the only thing on people’s minds at this year’s Sundance Film Festival, which comes to a close Sunday in Park City, Utah ( and online ).
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: With home prices and mortgage rates high, many families find the American dream out of reach
item.description: The Petersen family’s two-bedroom apartment in northern California is starting to feel small. Four-year-old Jerrik's toy monster trucks are everywhere in the 1,100-square-foot unit in Campbell, just outside of San Jose.
item.dc:creator: R.J. RICO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia mayor confirms 7 dead, including one on the ground, in medical transport jet crash
item.description: Six people on board a medical transport jet that crashed in Philadelphia were killed. That is according to Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KTLA 5

item.title: Deadly Eaton and Palisades fires 100% contained after 24 days
item.description: The deadly, wind-fueled Palisades and Eaton fires, which broke out 3½ weeks ago in Southern California, were declared fully contained Friday by the California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Author's story of coping after a wildfire resonates with community affected by latest LA-area fires
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — On a quiet summer evening in June 1990, Pico Iyer sat in his family home in Santa Barbara, California, when suddenly, he was surrounded by walls of flames five stories high.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bodycam footage shows moment Florida officers' attempt to restrain suspect goes horribly wrong
item.description: A Miami Beach Police officer will not face criminal charges after accidentally shooting a fellow cop while responding to a call on a busy Florida roadway.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats call for reinstatement of IGs fired by Trump
item.description: The lawmakers said that Congress did not receive the 30-day notice or the reason for the IG’s removals which took place at over a dozen agencies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: A number of U.S. government web pages changed or went dark Friday as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York doctor indicted for allegedly prescribing abortion pill to patient via telemedicine in Louisiana
item.description: A female New York doctor was recently indicted on felony charges for allegedly prescribing abortion medication to a patient via telemedicine in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Air traffic controller left early from work before crash: report
item.description: An air traffic controller at Reagan National Airport reportedly left early on Wednesday before the deadly collision between an American Airlines flight and a military helicopter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Special Report: Behind the stories we uncovered for Black History Month
item.description: Among the topics discussed is a deeper dive into our coverage of a surge in black-owned businesses from WHTM's Valerie Pritchett, WFLA's Deanne King delving into stories of everyday people working to fix systemic problems in black communities, and KSEE's Sydney Charles reporting on the role Buffalo
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Donald Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dick Button, Olympic great and voice of skating, dies at 95
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dick Button was more than the most accomplished men’s figure skater in history. He was one of his sport’s great innovators and promoters, the man responsible not only for inventing the flying camel spin but describing it to generations of fans.
item.dc:creator: BARRY WILNER and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas judge sets bond for accused serial rapist at $1 million
item.description: In the first Las Vegas case, Billingsley pretended to be a rideshare driver, according to the Clark County District Attorney's office.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, NewsNation

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: McAllen, Texas (AP) — A federal judge extended a court agreement on Thursday ensuring safe and sanitary conditions for migrant children in federal custody a day after U.S. Customs and Border Protection was set to begin self-monitoring.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: Court says banning gun sales to young adults under 21 is unconstitutional
item.description: BATON ROUGE, Louisiana (AP) — A U.S. appeals court on Thursday ruled against a federal law requiring young adults to be 21 to buy handguns, finding it violated the Second Amendment. The ruling, handed down by a panel of three judges on the conservative U.S. 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mid-air collision draws attention to military helicopter training, previous accidents
item.description: To learn to fly a military helicopter is to take a master class in multitasking. You need both hands and both feet to control the aircraft while keeping a close eye on the horizon and your altitude.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee GOP passes school voucher expansion bill with backing from Trump
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee lawmakers on Thursday approved legislation drastically expanding the number of families who can use taxpayer money on private schools regardless of income, a long-sought victory for Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flu activity high in Florida, CDC says
item.description: The map of flu activity across the United States continues to darken, both figuratively and literally, as case numbers spike upward in most states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI wrestles with a spike in sexual misconduct claims and male-dominated culture
item.description: An investigation by AP revealed that the incidents ranged from sexual assault to agents paying for sex at massage parlors.
item.dc:creator: Jim Mustian, KRON4

item.title: Amazon layoffs to impact dozens of workers, according to reports
item.description: (KRON) -- Amazon is reportedly cutting dozens of corporate jobs in its latest round of layoffs. The layoffs will impact people in Amazon's communications department, according to a report in Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Marianne Faithfull dead at 78
item.description: (KRON) -- Singer, actress and 1960s icon Marianne Faithfull has died at the age of 78. As a singer, Faithfull was best known for her 1964 debut single "As Tears Go By," and her acclaimed 1979 album, "Broken English.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: What we know about the DC plane crash victims
item.description: Among those killed were figure skaters, union members, and a couple en route to visit their child at college.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames DEI for weakening FAA in aftermath of Reagan National plane crash
item.description: President Trump on Thursday appeared to blame diversity initiatives at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for weakening safety following a deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth: Army battalion involved in DCA crash grounded for 48 hours
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said he has grounded the Army battalion involved in the operation of the Black Hawk helicopter with three soldiers that crashed into a passenger plane with more than 60 people on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, WFLA, NBC
